title: plumeria clusioides var. parviflora
intro: Plumeria clusioides var. parviflora, also known as the dwarf frangipani, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Mexico and Central America and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Plumeria clusioides var. parviflora include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
